heading: Assessment of science mission extensions
label: 30504 Assessment of science mission extensions
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)30504(a)(matter before par. (1)).42 U.S.C. 16654(a)(matter before par. (1)), (2), , .Pub. Amendments2017— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows:Pub. L. 115–10“(a) .—The Administrator shall carry out biennial reviews within each of the Science divisions to assess the cost and benefits of extending the date of the termination of data collection for those missions that have exceeded their planned mission lifetime.Assessment“(b) .—For those missions that have an operational component, the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration or any other affected agency shall be consulted and the potential benefits of instruments on missions that are beyond their planned mission lifetime taken into account.”Consultation and Consideration of Potential Benefits of Instruments on Missions

L. 115–10“(a) .—The Administrator shall carry out biennial reviews within each of the Science divisions to assess the cost and benefits of extending the date of the termination of data collection for those missions that have exceeded their planned mission lifetime.Assessment“(b) .—For those missions that have an operational component, the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration or any other affected agency shall be consulted and the potential benefits of instruments on missions that are beyond their planned mission lifetime taken into account.”Consultation and Consideration of Potential Benefits of Instruments on Missions

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Administrator shall carry out triennial reviews within each of the Science divisions to assess the cost and benefits of extending the date of the termination of data collection for those missions that exceed their planned missions’ lifetime.

children.heading: .—Considerations
children.label: 2 .—Considerations
children.text: In conducting an assessment under paragraph (1), the Administrator shall consider whether and how extending missions impacts the start of future missions.

children.heading: .—Consultation and Consideration of Potential Benefits of Instruments on Missions
children.label: b .—Consultation and Consideration of Potential Benefits of Instruments on Missions

children.text: consult with any affected Federal agency; and
take into account the potential benefits of instruments on missions that are beyond their planned mission lifetime.

children.heading: .—Reports
children.label: c .—Reports
children.text: The Administrator shall submit to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation of the Senate and the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology of the House of Representatives, at the same time as the submission to Congress of the Administration’s annual budget request for each fiscal year, a report detailing any assessment under subsection (a) that was carried out during the previous year.
